reasoning: Fabiano Caruana's rating on the most recent FIDE list is approximately 2789. A 1% increase would require a jump of nearly 28 rating points. In elite chess (2780+), gaining 28 points over a span of 1 to 3 months is exceedingly rare because expected scores are very high, leading to small gains for wins and large penalties for draws/losses. Furthermore, the first resolution date (Nov 16) is only 7 days after the knowledge cutoff, during which the official FIDE monthly rating list will not even update, guaranteeing the rating will not change. The empirical base rate for this exact reference class is 0.007. Constrained by the minimum allowable prediction (0.05) and the necessity for probabilities to approach 0.5 over longer horizons, the final estimates are set at the permitted lower bounds.
